seo.title: Building Workflow Automation with Azure AI Agent Service + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build workflow automation with Azure AI Agent Service + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.
keywords: Azure AI Agent Service workflow automation

content.intro: Azure AI Agent Service enables powerful workflow automation by combining Azure OpenAI models with enterprise-grade grounding, tool calling, and security controls for multi-step task execution. Integrating Vex as middleware ensures reliability by validating actions against enterprise policies, preventing token exhaustion, and enforcing idempotency in complex workflows. Guardrails are essential to mitigate risks like unvalidated tool outputs and outdated grounding data that can derail enterprise automation.

architectureOverview.heading: Workflow Automation Architecture with Azure AI Agent Service
architectureOverview.description:
Azure AI Agent Service orchestrates multi-step workflows using agent threads that maintain conversation state across API calls, conditional branching, and human-in-the-loop approvals. Each step involves tool execution (APIs, file search, Bing grounding), state management via thread tokens, and enterprise policy enforcement through Azure RBAC. The pipeline processes user requests through an agent executor that handles reasoning, action selection, and observation cycles until task completion or escalation.

Typical workflows include order processing (API calls + approval), document analysis (file search + grounding), or IT automation (conditional remediation). Vex integrates as middleware between the Azure agent executor and tool handlers to validate outputs, enforce idempotency, and provide rollback mechanisms before committing state changes.

Human-in-the-loop occurs via Azure approval workflows, with Vex ensuring actions are auditable and reversible before escalation.

risks.name: Tool execution results not validated against enterprise policies
risks.description: Agents execute API calls without checking data sensitivity or compliance rules
risks.mitigation: Vex policy validator scans tool outputs against enterprise PII/DLP rules before state commit

risks.name: Grounding data from Bing Search containing outdated information
risks.description: Bing returns stale enterprise data leading to incorrect workflow decisions
risks.mitigation: Vex freshness checker validates grounding data against internal knowledge cutoff dates

risks.name: File search returning Azure-specific metadata instead of content
risks.description: RAG returns storage metadata instead of document content, breaking analysis
risks.mitigation: Vex content extractor strips metadata and validates semantic relevance

risks.name: Agent thread token limits exceeded without graceful degradation
risks.description: Long-running threads hit context limits causing workflow failure
risks.mitigation: Vex token monitor summarizes thread state and triggers checkpoint/rollback

implementation.title: Basic Azure AI Agent Thread with Tool Calling
implementation.description: Initialize agent thread and execute workflow steps with API integration
implementation.code:
from azure.ai.projects import AgentClient
from vex_sdk import VexClient

client = AgentClient(credential=DefaultAzureCredential(), endpoint="https://your-project.openai.azure.com/")
vex = VexClient(api_key="vex-api-key")

thread = client.beta.threads.create()

# Add workflow instructions
client.beta.threads.messages.create(
    thread_id=thread.id,
    role="user",
    content="Process order #12345: validate inventory, check approval, call shipping API"
)

# Execute with Vex middleware
run = client.beta.threads.runs.create(
    thread_id=thread.id,
    assistant_id="your-agent-id",
    tool_resources={
        "code_interpreter": {},
        "retrieval": {
            "file_ids": ["file-id"],
            "vector_store_ids": ["vs-id"]
        }
    }
)

# Vex validation before tool execution
if run.status == "requires_action":
    validated_tools = vex.validate_tools(run.required_action.submit_tool_outputs.tool_calls)
    client.beta.threads.runs.submit_tool_outputs(
        thread_id=thread.id,
        run_id=run.id,
        tool_outputs=validated_tools
    )

implementation.title: TypeScript Agent Executor with Conditional Logic
implementation.description: Handle branching workflows with human-in-the-loop using Azure SDK

implementation.title: Python Human-in-the-Loop Approval Handler
implementation.description: Implement approval workflow with Vex permission validation
implementation.code:
from azure.ai.projects import AgentClient
import asyncio

async def handle_approval_workflow(thread_id: str, approval_required: bool):
    client = AgentClient(credential=DefaultAzureCredential())
    
    if approval_required:
        # Pause for human review
        message = client.beta.threads.messages.list(thread_id)
        print(f"Approval needed for: {message.data[0].content[0].text.value}")
        
        # Vex permission check
        approval = input("Approve? (y/n): ")
        if approval.lower() == 'y':
            client.beta.threads.messages.create(
                thread_id=thread_id,
                role="user",
                content="Approved - continue workflow"
            )
        else:
            # Vex rollback
            vex.rollback_thread(thread_id)
            return False
    
    run = client.beta.threads.runs.create(thread_id, assistant_id="agent-id")
    return True

vexIntegration.title: Grounding Data Freshness Validator (Python)
vexIntegration.description: Ensure Bing search and file grounding data is current
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import VexClient
import json

from typing import Dict, Any

vex = VexClient(api_key="your-vex-key")

def validate_grounding_data(tool_output: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    if tool_output.get('tool_name') == 'bing_search':
        # Extract search results
        results = json.loads(tool_output['content'])
        
        fresh_results = []
        for result in results:
            freshness = vex.validate_freshness(
                result['url'],
                result.get('publish_date'),
                cutoff_days=7  # Enterprise policy
            )
            
            if freshness.is_fresh:
                fresh_results.append(result)
            else:
                print(f"Stale data rejected: {result['title']}")
        
        tool_output['content'] = json.dumps(fresh_results)
    
    return tool_output
